sections.paragraph:
δῶμα, ατος, τό (=‘house’ and ‘room’ since Hom.) in our lit. the level surface of a flat roof, roof, housetop (also Babrius 5, 5; pap since III b.c., e.g. POxy 475, 22 [II a.d.]; 1641, 5; PTebt 241 verso; PGM 1, 56 and 75; 4, 2469 and 2712; LXX; JosAs 12:12 [prob. for δόματα]; Just., D. 21, 4 [for δόμασιν Ezk 20:26]). Proverbially κηρύσσειν ἐπὶ τῶν δ. proclaim on the housetops=quite publicly Mt 10:27; Lk 12:3 (cp. 2 Km 16:22 ἐπὶ τὸ δῶμα … κατʼ ὀφθαλμοὺς παντὸς Ἰσραήλ); ἀναβαίνειν ἐπὶ τὸ δ. go up to the roof Lk 5:19; Ac 10:9. ὁ ἐπὶ τοῦ δώματος the one who happens to be on the housetop Mt 24:17; Mk 13:15; Lk 17:31 (ἐπὶ τοῦ δ. as Jos., Ant. 6, 49). Cp. RMeister, SBBerlAk 1911, 7, 633; Ltzm., ZNW 20, 1921, 172; RAC III 517–57; BHHW 1, 311.—DELG. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Babrius Babrius , c. 200 a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 266
